properties.remark: Both lanes of Highway 35 was blocked with 2 boulders the size of office chairs. This area is the old burn scar Boulder 2700 from 2019. Another rock slide was observed 1.5 miles south of Yellow Bay but it did not make it to the road edge...there were some large boulders.

The Idaho County Sheriffs office posted on Facebook that Highway 162 near Fort Misery Rd was closed due to mud slides, rocks and trees. Dual Pol radar precipitation estimates show around 1.76 of rain fell in this location, further to the north and east it shows a bulls-eye of 2.80 inches. This would have fallen between 7 and 10 pm PDT from slow moving thunderstorms.
Mesonet station LPFI1 Lodgepole.

Multiple pine trees were uprooted by wind. One of the trees fell on top of a Jeep and demolished it. Facebook photos show a moist soil which could also have contributed to the trees to fall over with their shallow root systems.
Live powerline down over Highway 93 due to thunderstorm winds. Hamilton fire on the scene. Sheriff is closing the highway both southbound and northbound until further notice.

A witness described multiple living, mature Douglas Fir and Grand Fir trees snapped in half. After reviewing the radar data, it appears a microburst occurred. After the thunderstorm pulsed up over the headwaters of the American River basin, north of Elk City, it then dropped over the Selway River between Selway Falls and Glover Campground. For these healthy trees to be snapped in half, the wind speeds could have exceeded 70 mph. It was reported that some of the trees were positioned towards the west-southwest direction.
Avista Utilities reports 700 customers without power associated with thunderstorm wind damage.
Idaho County Sheriff Office reports closure of US Highway 12 near Kooskia to the Montana border due to rock slides and fallen trees.
Avista Utilities reports 775 customers without power associated with thunderstorm wind damage in Elk City. Time estimated via radar.
Idaho County Sheriff Office reports downed trees blocking Selway Road. Time estimated via radar.

*** 1 INJ *** Dozens of downed fir and pine trees, with several broken like matchsticks. Two damaged buildings, with injured USFS employee located inside cabin as tree fell onto the building. Severity of injury unknown at this time. Time of damage estimated via radar.
Pittsburg Landing RAWs report.
Several damaged and downed trees leading to power outage into the following morning.

Multiple trees with diameters of up to 2 feet fell due to high winds at the Wendover Campground, located west of Powell Idaho along US-12. One tree crushed a 5th wheel camper. Another tree crushed the top of a recent-year Jeep Grand Cherokee Laredo. Photos are found on the Nez Pierce/Clearwater National Forest Facebook page. Winds are estimated at 70 to 80 mph. Time is radar estimated.
Idaho Department of Transportation reports multiple trees and rocks on US Highway 12 from milepost 158 to the Idaho Montana border. Time estimated via radar.
Ravalli County Sheriff Office reports downed power lines on Sweathouse Creek Rd and 5th Avenue. Time estimated via radar.
Ravalli County Sheriff Office reports downed power lines on Red Crow Rd and Bumpy Lane. Time estimated via radar.

Video along US Highway 12 from Wendover Campground to Powell Ranger Station shows widespread significant tree damage. Wendover and Whitehouse Campgrounds hit especially hard and are closed until further notice. The storm was destructive for several miles east and west of Powell Ranger Station.
Idaho Department of Transportation reports downed trees on US 12, both directions from Road 564 to Beaver Ridge Road 369.
Ravalli County Sheriff Office reports downed tree leading to closure of Logan Lane. Time estimated via radar.
Report from mPING: 1-inch tree limbs broken; Shingles blown off.
APRSWXNET/CWOP Site ID MOMM8.
Missoula Electric Coop reports broken power poles along US Highway 12.
Missoula Electric Coop reports significant damage to powerlines across Allen Dr. Pleasant Meadows Ln. Vann Dr. Lolo Creek Dr. and Chickaman Ln. in the west Lolo area. Time estimated via radar.
Missoula Electric Coop reports damage to power lines from Lolo to Florence.
Report from mPING: 3-inch tree limbs broken; Power poles broken.
Over 200 total trees downed at Missoula Country Club and Larchmont Golf Course.

Missoula DNRC campus lost 52 mature ponderosa pine trees. Missoula city officials estimated 80 percent of city trees sustained damage with a conservative estimate of 1000 trees being uprooted and or splintered at the trunk.
Numerous power poles snapped including a metal transmission tower. Time estimated via radar.
56 trees downed at Linda Vista Golf Course.
Report from mPING: Trees uprooted or snapped; Roof blown off.
Missoula County Sheriff reports downed power lines across the Bitterroot River. Advised citizens to stay away from the river.
Missoula Electric Coop reports the most severe damage to the system along Mulan Road, with 42 broken power poles.

NWS Employees estimate wind gusts of 90-100 mph, based on damage. Large fir trees uprooted and snapped in half. Metal high voltage transmission lines bent over. Widespread damage to power infrastructure across the city caused emergency services to declare emergency travel only. Montana Governor issued disaster declaration on July 26 given widespread damage and power outages across the city.
Several large downed trees. Power outage.
Toppled 70 year old maple tree, along with other tree damage. Power and cell service out.
Gusts to 60+ mph has been occurring for ~20 minutes.

Mesonet station SMYI1 Salmon. A strong cold front initiated a thunderstorm over the mountains to the west. Radar imagery revealed the thunderstorm dropped its core by 6:13 pm which triggered the high winds.
